data.title: InterRent REIT: Solid And Stable, Hold Until You Are Unable
data.description:
All values are in CAD unless noted otherwise.

The residential landlord, InterRent Real Estate Investment Trust (TSX:IIP.UN:CA) got a "buy" rating from us a little over a couple of months ago. We found it a solid choice in the Canadian space and inexpensive to boot. We were so inspired by the REIT, that we even concluded that piece with what we think is a very, very, very decent rhyme (change our mind).

Our two-year price target for InterRent is $18 (yes, we expect the NAV to increase as well). With the current stock price at a little over $12, it has room to fly, and we rate it a buy.

Source: InterRent REIT: Solid And Inexpensive

We didn't just wax poetically about InterRent, we had committed to the cause by becoming buyers. It only made sense, seeing that we were even more optimistic about the value of the properties than the landlord (over a two year period though, to be fair). The REIT has appreciated by almost 13% since then.

This performance has more to do with the topic du jour today, than our stellar analytical skills. Without further ado, lets discuss the impending sale.

On May 27, InterRent made the above announcement. Carriage Hill Properties Acquisition Group is the face of the purchase and the entity is owed by the above noted CLVGroup and GIC. Under the terms of the offer, Carriage Hill assumes the net debt of the REIT. Alongside that, the unitholders will receive $13.55 in cash, a number that is derived using the following criteria:

  • 35% premium to InterRent's unaffected closing unit price on the TSX as of March 7, 2025, the last trading day prior to media speculation regarding the REIT, and a
  • 29% premium to InterRent's 90-day VWAP on the TSX as of May 26, 2025 Board of Trustees of InterRent (the "Board") unanimously recommends that unitholders vote in favor of the Transaction

Source: Press Release

The stock was trading around $11.75 just before the announcement, so the $13.55 brought cheers of joy from the REIT's investors (us included). Unsurprisingly, the stock price rose to meet the per unit sale price promptly.

Once the unitholders had adapted to this high, they gave a thought to the REIT's recently published net asset value, which was $16.23. The sale terms had a little something, something to assuage their FOMO too.

Agreement includes a "Go-Shop" period of 40 days during which InterRent may actively solicit, evaluate and enter into negotiations with third parties that express an interest in acquiring InterRent

Source: Press Release

The Go-Shop period started on May 28, 2025 and ended about a month ago, on July 6, 2025. We cannot say that the REIT did not make an effort.

During the Go-Shop Period, the REIT was permitted to actively solicit, facilitate and enter into negotiations with third parties that expressed an interest in acquiring the REIT. BMO Capital Markets ("BMO"), the REIT's financial advisor, contacted 85 potential buyers on behalf of the REIT. Nine of the potential buyers entered into confidentiality agreements with the REIT and were granted access to non-public information about the REIT. The Go-Shop Period expired at 11:59 p.m. ET on July 6, 2025. The REIT did not receive an Acquisition Proposal (as such term is defined in the Arrangement Agreement) during the Go-Shop Period.

Source: Press Release (emphasis ours)

85?? We wouldn't have even thought there were 30 potential buyers. Despite leaving no stone unturned, there was no new offer. While, no other offers materialized during this period, it gave the unitholders time to come to terms with the below NAV, but still decent sale price. Ourselves? We sold one-third of our units when the optimism was at its peak, around $13.70. It briefly went there soon after the announcement.

With the Go-Shop period in the rearview mirror, one of the final steps, unitholders voting is underway, with the deadline being August 21. If the majority say "yay", the transaction is expected to be completed late this year or early next.

The Arrangement was announced on May 27, 2025, and is expected to close in late 2025 or early 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary conditions, including Unitholder, court and regulatory approvals and approvals from Canada Mortgage and Housing Corporation ("CMHC") and certain of InterRent's lenders.

Source: Press Release

As is the case with these transactions, both parties have protection in place in the form of termination fees.

A termination fee of approximately $49 million or $79 million would be payable to the Purchaser under certain customary circumstances if the Arrangement Agreement is terminated during or after the Go-Shop Period, respectively. A reverse termination fee of approximately $89 million would be payable to the REIT if the Arrangement Agreement is terminated in certain circumstances.

Source: Press Release

It's Not Over Until It's Over

At the current price, it is a nice "hold" until the transaction gets completed. Assuming the bow is put on this package in 6 months, you have 31 cents of upside to target price and an additional 20 cents of dividends. That is a nice 7.5% plus annualized return from here. The odds of this not going through are fairly slim, but that is of course possible. That was one reason we dumped a third at a price over the buyout price. With many residential REITs moving lower in the interim, we are now ready to deploy into our favorite names with the cash raised.

Please note that this is not financial advice. It may seem like it or sound like it, but surprisingly, it is not. Investors are expected to do their own due diligence and consult a professional who knows their objectives and constraints. Most of our articles are not written for "buy and hold" investors. Our articles are definitely not written for investors who ignore capital preservation while focusing only on yield.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: PagerDuty Improves Operating Results Amid Acquirer Interest Report (Rating Upgrade)
data.description:
Investment Outlook

PagerDuty (NYSE:PD) has recently disappointed with its sequential revenue decline amid large customer churn.

I previously analyzed PD in February 2025 with a Sell outlook as it has traded growth rate for only marginally improved operating results.

Unsubstantiated reports have emerged that the company may be the target of an acquisition attempt.

While its topline revenue performance has worsened, its operating results have continued to slowly improve, resulting in better Rule of 40 performance.

With a potential acquirer circling, management producing encouraging operating improvements and a beaten-down valuation, I’m upgrading my outlook to a Hold.

PagerDuty’s Market And Approach

PagerDuty operates in the broader digital operations management market.

The company platform is designed to monitor IT systems for disruptions and outages, either as a standalone service or integrated with existing IT systems, and notify responsible parties for remediation.

PD’s platform is applicable to various sectors, such as software developers, financial services, customer service, healthcare, and government agencies.

Its service and software offerings include:

  • On-call management

  • Incident response

  • Runbook automation

  • Event intelligence

  • Automation actions

  • Customer service operations

  • Status pages

  • AI Agents

The digital operations management market was an estimated $39 billion in 2023 and is expected to reach $73.4 billion by 2030, according to a Grand View Research market research report.

The report forecasted a CAGR of 9.4% from 2024 to 2030.

This projected growth will likely be driven by ongoing digital transformation and the adoption of cloud and hybrid environment infrastructure.

PD sells its offerings via a fairly standard ‘land and expand’ approach, seeking to gain an initial foothold in each prospect, followed by expansion, cross-sell and up-selling efforts to grow its service offerings throughout each client.

The firm’s revenue contribution between the US and International is illustrated in the pie chart here, and has been stable in recent years:

Recent Financial Trends

Total revenue by quarter (columns) has declined sequentially due to enterprise customer downgrades and higher-than-normal churn. Operating income by quarter (red line) has improved slightly because of higher gross margin and lower SG&A and R&D costs as a function of revenue.

The degree of operating leverage has trended lower in recent periods on disappointing revenue performance.

Gross profit margin by quarter (blue line) has remained healthy, although management targets 84% to 86% range, which it is still at the bottom of. Selling and G&A expenses as a percentage of total revenue by quarter (orange line) have trended lower due to reduced personnel costs. R&D expenses as a function of revenue (purple line) have slid despite management’s continued emphasis on integrating generative AI technologies into the company's offerings.

Earnings per share (Diluted) have remained negative but have been trending unevenly toward breakeven in recent quarters.

(All data in the above charts is GAAP.)

For balance sheet results, PD ended the quarter with $597.1 million in cash, equivalents and short-term investments and $451.4 million in total debt, of which $57.5 million was due within twelve months.

In the past 12 months, PD’s stock price has dropped by 12%, severely underperforming the benchmark iShares Expanded Technology-Software ETF’s (IGV) rise of 41%, as the chart indicates below.

Below is a helpful financial and operating metrics table for PD and includes forward and trailing figures:

Metric

Amount

EV/Sales - FWD

2.7

EV/EBITDA Multiple - FWD

11.3

Rev. Growth Rate Est. - FWD

7.3%

2025 FWD EPS Estimate

$0.97

Price/Sales - TTM

3.0

Revenue Growth Rate - TTM

8.6%

Net Income Margin - TTM

-7.8%

EBITDA Margin - TTM

-6.1%

Market Capitalization

$1,440,000,000

Enterprise Value

$1,320,000,000

Operating Cash Flow

$119,910,000

EPS (Fully Diluted)

-$0.40

Free Cash Flow per Share - TTM

$1.20

Seeking Alpha Quant Score

Hold: 3.32

(Source - Seeking Alpha)

The company's Rule of 40 performance has improved in recent quarters, but still needs substantial improvement going forward:

Capital Allocation

Over the trailing twelve months, free cash flow was $117.1 million, and capital expenditures were $2.8 million.

However, the company paid $119 million in stock-based compensation in the last four quarters, so free cash flow net of SBC was negative ($1.9 million).

The net dollar decrease in stock outstanding for the last four quarters was valued at $113.5 million, so the company has been retiring almost all of its issued SBC.

SBC as a percentage of revenue over the trailing twelve-month period was 25%, a very high figure for a company that has been around as long as PagerDuty has.

Effectively, the buybacks are hiding dilution to shareholders.

Management’s plans for capital allocation in relation to shares will be to continue to opportunistically buy back shares from its current $150 million authorization, with a nod toward limiting SBC.

Why I’m Neutral On PagerDuty

PD’s sales efforts fell short in the most recent quarter and management has committed to rectifying that situation, but that may take several quarters to accomplish.

The company hired a new Chief Customer Officer, to better manage post-sale engagement in large enterprise customers in order to improve retention, which has been a source of revenue disappointment.

PD’s annual recurring revenue is growing at only 7% YoY compared to 8% for overall revenue, not a good sign.

It is also seeing greater churn in the SMB segment, a worrying signal.

A comparative bright spot is its international segment, which grew at 11% YoY.

Management’s current growth strategy involves enhancing its partnerships, steadily expanding in international, and US federal government agency authorizations.

The chart below shows the frequency of various negative keywords in the most recent conference call between management and analysts.

The graphic highlights that PD is seeing challenges with macroeconomic uncertainties slowing decision-making and demand for its offerings.

It also has faced internal ‘execution challenges’ within its sales & marketing efforts.

Regarding valuation, the market is valuing PD at a forward EV/Sales multiple of around 2.7x on an estimated NTM revenue growth rate of 6% against a median Meritech Capital SaaS Index implied ARR growth rate of approximately 14% (Source).

Also, the benchmark index indicates a recent median Net Dollar Retention Rate of 110% vs. PD’s NRR of 104%.

The index of publicly held SaaS application software companies showed a forward EV/Revenue multiple median of around 5.3x on June 27, 2025, as the chart shows here:

So, by comparison, PD is currently being valued by public investors at a significant discount to the broader index as of June 27, 2025, likely due to its lower growth rate, dollar-based net retention rate and operating loss generation.

Looking ahead, management reduced its forward estimate for fiscal 2026 topline revenue growth, to 6%, and promised remedial action on its sales efforts.

Also, recent reports indicate the company may be the target of takeover interest.

With hopeful operating improvements, an improving Rule of 40 trend, beaten-down valuation and the potential for acquirer interest, I’m upgrading my outlook to a Hold.

data.title: Lucid: New Low Possible As Guidance Cut
data.description:
After the bell on Tuesday, we received second quarter results from Lucid (NASDAQ:LCID). The luxury electric vehicle name has been one of the most disappointing companies in this space over the years, as it still struggles to sell just a few thousand vehicles each quarter. Unfortunately for investors, I was right in my prediction that yearly guidance could be cut, which could result in a new low for shares this year.

Previous coverage of the name

My most recent article on Lucid came about a month ago, after the company reported disappointing Q2 production and delivery numbers. With the first six months of the year in the books, it looked like there would need to be a major production ramp in the second half of the year, something that I believed provided a meaningful risk to management's previous guidance.

Lucid shares had rallied mid-teens percentage wise since my previous article (to Tuesday's close), compared to a small gain for the S&P 500. A lot of the rally could be attributed to the announced partnership with Uber (UBER), but a significant portion of the gains on that news has since been eliminated. With a terrible financial structure and very limited deliveries, Lucid has remained a sell in my opinion. I've been bearish on the name since the summer of 2022, at which point shares traded for more than $18.

A look at Q2 results

For the quarter, Lucid reported revenues of $259.4 million. While this was up almost 30% over the year ago period, the company had reported more than 38% delivery growth, so selling prices came down a bit. The number also missed street estimates by more than $5 million, the second straight miss on the top line and 8th miss in the past 11 quarters. While street revenue estimates have continued to new lows recently as seen below, they probably could decline further in the coming months.

Unfortunately, the revenue miss wasn't the worst news here. Despite the record quarterly delivery print, gross margins weakened sequentially by more than 7 percentage points to minus 105%. That was significant improvement over the prior year period in percentage terms, but this was only a function of increased revenues, as negative gross margin dollars actually increased. We've now seen two quarters in a row where gross margins have stepped back. Additionally, while operating margins were better than a year ago, the operating loss increased by over $15 million to more than $803 million. That gap was even wider if you take out a $20 million restructuring charge that was taken in Q2 2024.

In terms of annual guidance, management did cut its forecast. The new expectation is for production of 18,000 to 20,000 vehicles, down from 20,000 vehicles previously. The Gravity SUV ramp seemingly is taking more time than expected, and there has been a shift in sentiment in recent years against luxury electric vehicles in the US. As a reminder, when the company went public in 2021, the forecast was for 135,000 deliveries this year, of which 128,000 was to come from the currently on sale Air and Gravity vehicles. Lucid is set to launch its mid-size platform next year that will feature much lower price points, and hopefully much higher volumes.

Balance sheet and cash flow update

With significant ongoing losses, Lucid has burned through billions of cash over the years. In Q2 2025, the company burned over $1 billion, significantly higher than the $741 million seen a year earlier, despite a more than $50 million reduction in capital expenditures. As seen in the chart below, the company's cash and investments total is at its lowest point in more than two years.

The company's net cash balance is even lower, as it has over $2 billion of debt on the balance sheet. Lucid ended the quarter with a reported $4.86 billion in total liquidity, but it wouldn't surprise me to see another equity or debt raise sometime soon. At the end of June, more than 3.03 billion shares were outstanding, almost double the amount seen in late 2021.

The premium valuation remains

While Lucid is expected to show some strong revenue percentage growth in the coming years, it also remains a very expensive stock currently. As the chart below shows, only Tesla has a price-to-sales ratio, based on current respective 2025 analyst estimates, that is higher than Lucid. The other names in this space I've used as a comparison are VinFast Auto Ltd. (VFS), Rivian Automotive, Inc. (RIVN), XPeng Inc. (XPEV), BYD Company Limited (OTCPK:BYDDF), NIO Inc. (NIO), Polestar Automotive Holding UK PLC (PSNY), and ZEEKR Intelligent Technology Holding Limited (ZK). These numbers were as of Tuesday's close.

If you average the 8 names other than Lucid, the result is about 2.55 times each's expected 2025 revenue. Lucid finished Tuesday going for about 4.75 times, a significant premium to this comparison group. If we were to exclude Tesla, however, that average drops to just 1.42 times, and these numbers don't include traditional US automakers like Ford Motor Company (F) or General Motors Company (GM) that trade at about 0.25 times their expected revenues for this year. The average Lucid price target as of Tuesday afternoon was $2.60, implying high single digit percentage upside, but that number had been dramatically cut from almost $30 just three years ago.

Final thoughts and recommendation

Lucid announced another set of extremely disappointing results on Tuesday, reporting a sizable revenue miss and increased losses. Shares tumbled in the after hours session as management also cut production guidance for the year, and cash burn in the quarter again topped $1 billion. The company continues to struggle mightily on almost every financial metric, and another capital raise may be coming soon.

With these results being terrible and guidance being cut, I have to keep my sell rating on the stock. I fear that more dilution is coming, and management hasn't shown any ability to improve results significantly over time. Until revenues can get to a much higher base and losses and cash burn come down substantially, it would not surprise me if shares trend lower, possibly putting their sub $2 all-time low in play again this year.

data.title: Alphabet Continues To Be A Steal
data.description:
In my previous article on Alphabet Inc. (NASDAQ:GOOGL), I declared that the stock was a steal and upgraded it to a strong buy. I cited strong top and bottom line growth as well as significant undervaluation as the reasons for my bullish stance. So far, it has been quite a good call with the stock advancing around 20% since then. In the below analysis, it is shown that the stock continues to be a steal. Top line growth is accelerating and operating margin is holding strong. The most promising sign of all is the strength in their Google Cloud segment. While CAPEX is of course weighing on FCFs in the near term, investors should have a long run view on the company as AI investments are likely to pay off handsomely in my opinion. While the P/E ratio has expanded moderately since my previous article, I believe the stock continues to be undervalued and so I reiterate my strong buy rating on Alphabet.

Revenue Growth Resilient

Alphabet reported their 2025 Q2 earnings in late July and top line results are robust at first glance. They reported that consolidated revenues grew 14% YoY to $96.4 billion as they had "robust momentum across the business." The top line bested estimates by $2.39 billion to show significant outperformance. Revenue growth has rebounded for two straight quarters now and is at one of the highest levels of the past three years. While a growth rate in the low to mid teens percentage range doesn't seem all that impressive, for a company of this size to be sustaining this type of growth is actually quite impressive. Also note that the growth rate of 14% was the same as in the prior year period. Therefore, they have been able to sustain robust growth with a significantly larger growth base.

Operating Margin Stable

For operating income, Alphabet also reported a 14% increase YoY to $31.271 billion. This implies that operating margin did not change. Operating margin was steady at 32.4%, signalling strong efficiency but there are some developments that are worthy of discussion. In the above diagram, you can see that operating expenses actually increased by a whopping 20% YoY. The reason that operating margin was able to stay stable was actually because cost of revenues only grew by 10%. Gross margin is not a metric that Alphabet likes to report but clearly, the figure would have shown significant improvement. As for operating expenses, there are some encouraging signs and some minor worrying signs. Research & Development expenses grew 16% YoY to $13.808 billion but for investors that is a signal that the company is continuing to invest for the future. Therefore, I actually see this as a positive signal. On the other hand, the 65% increase in General and Administrative expenses to $5.209 billion indicates a notable decrease in corporate efficiency that investors should take note of. While it's not a major concern currently, it would be wise to keep an eye on this metric in the quarters ahead.

Segment Analysis

Google Services

Alphabet's Google Services segment saw very steady results. For Q2, revenues grew 12% YoY to $82.543 billion. That's the same growth rate as in the year ago quarter. Looking more closely, you can see that their Google Search & Other revenues grew at a healthy 12% YoY pace, suggesting that AI hasn't disrupted the company's near monopoly on search like many had feared. As you can see in the above image, operating margin was also unchanged from the prior year period and so overall, their Google Services business exhibited resilient results.

Google Cloud

While Google Services was calm and steady, Google Cloud is seeing far more exciting results. Revenues grew 32% YoY to $13.624 billion and this growth rate is a notable step up from 2024 Q2's 29%. They stated that this was due to "growth in Google Cloud Platform (GCP) across core GCP products, AI Infrastructure, and Generative AI Solutions." Also note that this is a considerable acceleration from the previous quarter's 28% growth as well and so it seems they are making significant inroads. Furthermore, operating income jumped by 141% and margin soared 9.4 percentage points. That is a stunning improvement. While Google Cloud remains by far the smaller of the two segments, the trajectory of growth and efficiency should be encouraging for investors.

EPS & Operating Cash Flow

Despite operating margin being unchanged YoY and revenues only growing in the low teens percentage range, diluted EPS grew by 22.22% to $2.31. Like revenues, this figure also beat expectations as they surpassed estimates by a healthy $0.11. EPS growth has dropped off a bit from the previous quarter but overall, there's nothing to really complain about regarding the company's profitability. For operating cash flow, they saw a 4.16% increase to $27.747 billion. While that is still acceptable, the relatively sluggish growth indicates that cash generation hasn't been overly strong. That could be a mild concern especially with CAPEX going through the roof. We turn to this discussion next.

CAPEX Soars

It is no secret that hyper-scalers like Alphabet are pouring money into AI infrastructure. As you can see in their Google Cloud segment, these investments are already driving financial results for the company. As AI continues to become increasingly popular as well as important, companies like Alphabet are going to need to spend to remain competitive or maybe perhaps to gain market share in the insanely competitive space of cloud computing. For Q2, CAPEX soared 70% YoY to $22.446 billion. This is the highest growth rate since 2024 Q2. Due to the reasons that I just stated, I believe that as of now it seems that these investments are justified and are necessary for future growth.

Just earlier, I stated that the sluggish growth in operating cash flows could be a potential concern. The above charts show why that's the case. FCFs plunged 60.6% YoY to $5.301 billion in Q2. This is the worst plunge in three years and FCF hit three year lows. This is of course due to the CAPEX spending discussed above. Therefore, in the near term, capital returns to shareholders may be negatively impacted. In Q2, they decreased their share repurchases by 13% YoY to $13.638 billion. If operating cash flow generation remains weak and CAPEX continues to grow, the company may have to decrease buybacks further in the near future. However, oftentimes short term pain is needed for long term gain. At the expense of near term returns, the company is obviously ambitiously investing for the future and so in the long run, I would expect the payoff to be handsome for shareholders. This discussion is not intended to be criticism of their spending but rather a display of where their cash is going.

Valuation Still Cheap

The P/E ratio for Alphabet saw sharp contraction early in the year but has recouped some of those declines in recent months. In my previous article, the multiple was at a reading of 18.27 and as shown above, it has expanded to a current reading of 21.45. I believe this continues to show undervaluation.

While EPS growth slowed a bit from the previous quarter, there are many indications of strong fundamentals. For example, revenue growth accelerated and operating margin continued to be robust. Furthermore, Google Search growth dispels fears of AI disruption. However, the greatest sign of all is in their all-important Google Cloud segment. As discussed in the segment analysis, revenue growth is accelerating and operating income soared 141% YoY. Operating margin nearly doubled. I believe it is becoming increasingly clear that AI investments are paying off for the company and Google Cloud has huge potential to re-accelerate growth for the company as a whole. With the long term outlook of Alphabet looking quite bright currently, I believe the P/E ratio still shows that the valuation is attractive.

Note that in GAAP terms, the multiple is at just a 5% premium to the Communication Services sector median of 20.38. With the company being one of the frontrunners in AI, I believe a much larger premium can be justified and so continued multiple expansion is likely from here.

Concluding Remarks

In my previous article, I stated that Alphabet was a steal. I believe that continues to be the case. There is honestly not much to complain about in their Q2 results. As mentioned just earlier, top line growth accelerated, operating margin remained robust and Google Cloud was a clear highlight for the company. The only blemish is likely weaker than desired operating cash flows and with CAPEX increasing significantly, FCFs plunged. As discussed earlier, the company is sacrificing near term shareholder returns for a bright future and I believe that is well justified. Lastly, while the valuation may be slightly less attractive than in my previous article, the P/E ratio continues to show undervaluation. As a result, I have decided to reiterate my strong buy rating on Alphabet.

data.title: IREN's Bitcoin Engine And The AI Cloud Opportunity
data.description:
IREN Limited (NASDAQ:IREN) is still a long term Hold at the highs. Continued aggressive growth, a transition to profitability and an asset light balance sheet led to a run up in valuations in the past quarter. The outlook for its core Bitcoin mining business is good for the upcoming earnings. The business may remain supported beyond the next quarter as well, but my Hold thesis hinges on IREN's pivot to AI Cloud.

I feel the timing of the pivot is great. At a time when the Bitcoin segment should go through a relatively strong phase, a transition to AI Cloud will be more seamless than in a tough environment for Bitcoin. The AI Cloud transition will have room and legs to grow in this regime. However, investors should remain cautious while the execution delivers on the growing AI Cloud business over the next couple of years.

The Growth Story

IREN is still in its nascent growth phase for the AI Cloud business. The company's overall revenue growth is explosive in triple digits for the past couple of quarters. That is because the Bitcoin mining business has expanded in Q3 2025 due to mining capacity expansion and better utilization before the pause (management has indicated a pause in mining expansion to foray into AI Cloud).

Going ahead I expect the legacy Bitcoin business to continue to benefit from a supportive Bitcoin regime and a rally that took place in Q4. However my thesis hinges critically on the AI Cloud foray and growth in the segment. It would be great optics and supportive financially too, if the Bitcoin business supports the transition, but triple digit growth may be challenged, particularly as capacity pause and business emphasis changes play out.

The Transition Scope and Path

AI Cloud is now only ~2% of the overall revenues. In Q3 2025, AI Cloud clocked $3.6m in revenues, compared to ~$140m from the Bitcoin mining business. In such a mix, the Bitcoin business' trough could very well drag revenue growth from the triple digit levels to near zero or even negative over the next few quarters. However, I am not looking at that as a critical and long term valuation definition. As long as the topline keeps itself stable with fluctuations, The AI Cloud story could grow through such troughs.

The initial signs from the AI Cloud business are very encouraging. The revenue, though small now, is scaling at 33% QoQ as per the last earnings report. Hardware profit margins are very strong at 97%. A mixed contract duration of up to 3 years gives visibility of ~$28m contracted revenue run rate annually. So while the actual revenues are still in single digits, the run rate pick up is already significant for the entire business' revenues.

As per June 2025 updates, the AI Cloud business continues to scale well. If IREN utilizes the Horizon 1 buildout efficiently, the AI Cloud revenues could reach $100m in a couple of years by conservative estimates (30% QoQ growth now plus the added capacity from Horizon 1 and later from Horizon 2). At those levels, the topline with a far better margin profile should start influencing IREN's valuations.

The Ask from the Bitcoin Business

The Bitcoin business has been growing through a purple patch with record revenue growth as seen before at decent margins. Cash balance is also strong and debt levels are minimal. This should enable the company to divert capex into AI Cloud expansion without overleveraging.

IREN identifies $250m as the funding required in 2025 to expand to 50 EH/s, completion of Horizon 1 and Sweetwater substation and development works. If Bitcoin prices remain supportive, as I expect, or even correct by 10-20%, the EBITDA covers the funding ask comfortably, as per IREN's own estimates. Which means sufficient and comfortable cash backed AI expansion till it stands on its own.

The progress in AI Cloud is therefore now squarely dependent on the execution. Which entails continued AI demand for GPU and compute capacity. I will be watching for key contract signings and hardware deployments to back the capacity created in 2025.

Valuation Expectations

Bitcoin is anyway a high gross margin business clocking 80%+ generally. I expect the AI Cloud business to deliver close to 70% gross margins as is commonly seen elsewhere. A ramped up AI Cloud business also smoothens the EBITDA profile and decouples it from Bitcoin price fluctuations. The path to stability in AI Cloud could lead to short term net margin fluctuations, as Bitcoin price changes continue to impact that as much as AI investments and R&D as data center costs hit before AI Cloud revenues ramp up. However, this is funded by existing cash and future cash flows, not dilutive equity or debt raises.

The current levels of EV to forward revenues at ~4.4x is slightly elevated to recent historical averages. The Bitcoin led growth and profitability trends alone justify the multiple expansion. I feel the markets are not yet fully baking in the upside narrative the AI Cloud augmentation brings to the table. As and when the AI Cloud execution shows more promise, I expect further rerating for IREN. But near term valuations may still be dictated by the Bitcoin story - the outlook for which is not weak, but open to stagnation or minor corrections from here on.

IREN a Long Term Hold

IREN is not yet an outright Buy as I am awaiting further traction and execution around the AI Cloud business. Some more capacity utilization of the new facilities (Horizon 1) will get me interested for fresh Buys. Till then, investors may see valuations and share prices react to near term volatility of the core Bitcoin results and lumpy nature of progress in AI Cloud.

I recommend a Hold on IREN and keep an eye out for the funding status versus progress made in the AI Cloud ramp up. As Bitcoin mining expansion pauses, that could mean monitoring for sharp corrections in Bitcoin prices or executional issues in the core business. I believe, the inflection point for IREN is not very far off, when the markets start valuing IREN as a Cloud AI business and valuations start respecting that narrative. Till then, existing investors should keep a longer term vision and only add to positions as more progress is evident.
